name: The Async Abyss

enemies.name: undefined
enemies.description: Is not a function. Is not an object. Is not anything.

enemies.name: NaN
enemies.description: Not a Number, but typeof says it's a number. Classic.

enemies.name: Callback Demon
enemies.description: Nested 12 levels deep. Each level angrier than the last.

enemies.name: Unhandled Rejection
enemies.description: A promise that was never caught. Now it catches you.

enemies.name: Event Loop Blocker
enemies.description: while(true) in the main thread. Everything freezes.

bosses.name: node_modules/
bosses.description: 2.4 GB. 847,000 files. You can't delete it. It IS the project now.

bosses.name: The Framework Churn
bosses.description: React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, Qwik — it's already obsolete.

traps.name: Type Coercion
traps.description: [] + {} === '[object Object]'. You take damage from the confusion.

traps.name: Lost 'this' Context
traps.description: 'this' points somewhere unexpected. It always does.

traps.name: npm audit
traps.description: 47 vulnerabilities found. 12 critical. No fixes available.

empty: Package.json files are scattered everywhere, each with conflicting versions.
The walls are papered with npm deprecation warnings.
A babel config so complex it bends spacetime.
Transpiled output nobody was ever meant to read.
rest: A node_modules-free zone. The air is light.
A clean TypeScript project with strict mode enabled.
Someone left a working webpack config. Miraculous.
treasure: A dependency with zero vulnerabilities. Priceless.
A working tsconfig.json with no 'any' types in sight.
An npm package that hasn't been deprecated yet.

floor_flavor: Unhandled promise rejections echo through the void.
A faint 'use strict' is carved into the entrance.
The floor is littered with abandoned left-pad alternatives.
boss_clues: The node_modules directory grows visibly deeper...
You hear the grinding of a thousand webpack loaders.
Framework logos flash and change on every surface.
